meta.name: Vacademy
meta.headline:
every feature.
one platform.
meta.subhead: Vacademy unifies LMS, assessments, live classrooms, CRM & admissions, marketing, communication, finance, HR and a full AI studio — so coaching institutes, schools and edtech companies can run everything from one place, under their own brand.
meta.terminologyNote: Every term below (Course, Level, Session, Batch, Learner, …) is configurable per institute via Settings → Naming Settings — your clients can rename them to match their own vocabulary (e.g. "Course" → "Program", "Learner" → "Student").

pillars.name: Learning Management (LMS)
pillars.tagline: Author rich courses and deliver them beautifully on every device
pillars.description: Vacademy's Learning Management pillar covers everything from building a course to a learner finishing it: a full course library, flexible 2-to-5 level hierarchies, and a slide-based editor supporting more than a dozen content types including video, PDFs, quizzes, code labs, Jupyter notebooks and SCORM. Content can be dripped, reused across batches, put through editorial approval, and protected against leaks — while learners consume it in a distraction-free player with true progress tracking, a personalized home dashboard, and automatically issued completion certificates.

features.name: Course Builder & Course Library
features.tagline: Create, organize and sell every course from one library
features.description: A central library where your team creates and manages every offering — classic courses, memberships, products or services — all on the same engine. Each course carries rich, marketing-ready details: description, learning outcomes, target audience, tags, preview image, banner and promo media. Search, filters, sorting and pagination keep even the largest catalogs manageable, and teaching teams are set up from day one with instructor invitations.

subFeatures.name: Guided course creation wizard
subFeatures.description: Step-by-step setup for name, description, learning outcomes, about section, target audience, tags, preview image, banner and promo media (image, uploaded video or YouTube link) with built-in image cropping.

subFeatures.name: Four offering types
subFeatures.description: Create each offering as a Course, Membership, Product or Service — the same engine sells classic courses, recurring memberships or one-off services.

subFeatures.name: Bulk course creation
subFeatures.description: Create many courses in a single operation instead of one at a time.

subFeatures.name: Course cards with live stats
subFeatures.description: Library cards show ratings, instructors with photos, tags and optionally the count of actively enrolled learners per course.

subFeatures.name: Search, filter and sort
subFeatures.description: Find courses by keyword, level, tag or owning staff member; sort results and page through large catalogs.

subFeatures.name: Edit and delete with safeguards
subFeatures.description: Update course details anytime; deletion asks for confirmation and supports removing multiple courses at once.

subFeatures.name: Faculty-scoped views
subFeatures.description: Teachers with restricted access see only their own courses, with irrelevant filters hidden automatically.

subFeatures.name: Instructor invitations
subFeatures.description: Invite co-instructors onto a course while creating it, so teaching teams are in place from the start.

features.name: Flexible Course Hierarchy
features.tagline: 2-to-5 level course structures that match how you teach
features.description: Structure courses with 2, 3, 4 or 5 levels of depth — from a simple chapter list up to full Level → Session → Subject → Module → Chapter trees — instead of being forced into a flat lesson list. Every layer can be added, renamed, reordered by drag-and-drop, copied, moved and deleted. Academic sessions (year or semester) and levels (class or grade) are optional and switch on or off per institute.

subFeatures.name: Selectable course depth
subFeatures.description: Choose a 2, 3, 4 or 5-level structure per course, or fix a default depth institute-wide so all courses stay consistent.

subFeatures.name: Levels management
subFeatures.description: Add, update and delete levels (e.g. Class 9, Beginner/Advanced) with durations and thumbnails.

subFeatures.name: Academic sessions
subFeatures.description: Create, edit and delete academic sessions (e.g. 2026-27) and map level/session combinations into batches.

subFeatures.name: Session-to-session content rollover
subFeatures.description: Roll a whole session's content forward into a new academic session in one action — no rebuilding each year.

subFeatures.name: Subjects and modules
subFeatures.description: Add, update, reorder and delete subjects and their modules, each with names and thumbnails.

subFeatures.name: Chapters with copy and move
subFeatures.description: Create and edit chapters, drag to reorder, and copy or move a chapter with all its slides to another module, course or batch.

subFeatures.name: Outline vs structure view
subFeatures.description: Choose whether learners and staff see a course as a flat outline or a nested structure, with sections expanded or collapsed by default.

features.name: Slide-Based Content Authoring
features.tagline: Every lesson is a slide — 12+ content types, one editor
features.description: Chapters are built from slides — individual learning units that can be a video, PDF, rich document, quiz, question, assignment, assessment, audio track, SCORM package, code editor, Jupyter notebook, Scratch project or interactive presentation. Authors work in a single editor with a chapter sidebar, adding, reordering, renaming, copying and moving slides freely, with full version history and engagement stats on every slide.

subFeatures.name: 12+ slide types from one menu
subFeatures.description: Add PDF, PPT presentation, rich document, video (upload, YouTube or Vimeo), question, quiz, assignment, assessment, presentation, Jupyter notebook, Scratch project, audio, code editor and SCORM slides from a single add menu.

subFeatures.name: Draft / publish lifecycle
subFeatures.description: Slides are authored as drafts and explicitly published; draft and published content are stored separately so edits never leak early, and slides can be unpublished again.

subFeatures.name: Drag-and-drop reordering
subFeatures.description: Reorder slides in the chapter sidebar with drag-and-drop; the new order saves instantly.

subFeatures.name: Copy and move slides
subFeatures.description: Copy or move any slide to a different chapter, module, subject or course — ideal for reusing lessons across batches.

subFeatures.name: Version history and restore
subFeatures.description: Every save is captured in a content history timeline; authors can view past versions of a slide and restore any of them with one click.

subFeatures.name: Slide-level engagement stats
subFeatures.description: An activity panel shows which learners viewed or submitted on each slide, flags late submissions, and exports all submissions to CSV.

subFeatures.name: In-context doubt resolution
subFeatures.description: Learner doubts raised on a slide appear in a sidebar where staff can reply, assign a teacher, mark resolved or delete.

subFeatures.name: Read-time analytics by content type
subFeatures.description: Slide counts and estimated read time per content type help gauge course length at a glance.

features.name: Interactive Course Player
features.tagline: One player for video, documents, quizzes, code and more
features.description: Learners consume content slide by slide in a distraction-free player with a full course-tree sidebar. The player natively renders more than a dozen content types — video, rich documents, PDFs, slide decks, audio, quizzes, assignments, code editors, notebooks, Scratch, SCORM and whiteboards — and always remembers exactly where the learner left off, down to the video second or PDF page.

subFeatures.name: Every slide type, natively rendered
subFeatures.description: Videos, documents, PDFs, presentation decks, audio, questions, quizzes, assignments, code editors, Jupyter notebooks, Scratch projects, SCORM packages and whiteboard slides all play inside one consistent player.

subFeatures.name: Course tree navigation
subFeatures.description: A collapsible sidebar shows the full Subject → Module → Chapter → Slide tree with per-item completion indicators and free navigation.

subFeatures.name: Resume where you left off
subFeatures.description: Every slide records progress, so learners resume at the exact video second or document page they left — on any device.

subFeatures.name: Custom video player
subFeatures.description: Plays YouTube, Vimeo and institute-uploaded videos with speed control and watch-position tracking.

subFeatures.name: Rich documents with math and diagrams
subFeatures.description: Rich-text lesson pages render cleanly, including math formulas and diagrams, in a focused reading view.

subFeatures.name: PDF viewer with page tracking
subFeatures.description: The in-app PDF viewer tracks pages read, so completion reflects how much of a document was actually studied.

subFeatures.name: AI-generated video lessons
subFeatures.description: Plays AI-produced lesson videos with synchronized visuals and narration, including a live progress display while a video is still generating.

subFeatures.name: Animated interactive lessons
subFeatures.description: Animated HTML lessons can embed live code editors, Scratch projects or Jupyter notebooks directly inside the lesson.

subFeatures.name: Embedded assessments
subFeatures.description: Full assessments can be launched from within a course chapter without leaving the learning flow.

subFeatures.name: End-of-course feedback slide
subFeatures.description: An automatic feedback step at the end of a course collects the learner's rating and comments.

features.name: Video Lessons with In-Video Questions
features.tagline: Videos that check understanding, not just play
features.description: Add video lessons by uploading files or linking YouTube, Vimeo or Google Drive videos. Authors drop questions at exact timestamps so playback pauses and learners must answer before continuing — turning passive watching into active learning. Behind the scenes the platform tracks genuine watch time, pauses and skips, giving you trustworthy engagement data instead of simple view counts.

subFeatures.name: Multiple video sources
subFeatures.description: Upload video files to secure cloud storage or link YouTube, Vimeo and Google Drive videos.

subFeatures.name: Timestamped question authoring
subFeatures.description: Insert questions at any point in the timeline, edit each question's time frame, preview it, and manage the full question list per video.

subFeatures.name: Pause-and-answer playback
subFeatures.description: The video pauses at preset moments and shows a question the learner answers in place before playback resumes.

subFeatures.name: True watch-time tracking
subFeatures.description: Records actual watched intervals, pause counts and jumps — not just 'opened the video' — for accurate completion data.

subFeatures.name: Video + notes split screen
subFeatures.description: Pair a video with a side-by-side document or whiteboard for follow-along notes.

subFeatures.name: Per-slide activity logs
subFeatures.description: Every learning interaction — video watched, PDF page read, quiz attempted — is logged and powers learner progress reports and engagement insights.

features.name: Quiz, Question & Survey Slides
features.tagline: Auto-graded practice woven into every chapter
features.description: Drop standalone questions, multi-question quizzes or surveys directly between lessons. Question types include single-choice, multiple-choice, true/false, numeric, one-word and long-answer — including comprehension variants built on a shared passage. Objective questions grade themselves instantly, scores feed into learner progress, and question slides can also run as surveys to collect feedback inside the course.

subFeatures.name: Six question types
subFeatures.description: MCQ single answer, MCQ multiple answers, true/false, numeric, one-word and long answer — plus comprehension versions of MCQ and numeric questions built on a passage.

subFeatures.name: Automatic evaluation
subFeatures.description: Objective types are auto-graded against configured correct answers; long answers can be collected for manual review.

subFeatures.name: Marks and negative marking
subFeatures.description: Set marks per question with optional negative marking, and allow or block skipping per question.

subFeatures.name: Explanations and media
subFeatures.description: Attach an explanation shown after answering and add images and media to questions and options.

subFeatures.name: Survey mode
subFeatures.description: Run question slides as surveys with no right answer to collect learner feedback inside the course.

subFeatures.name: Preview as a learner
subFeatures.description: Preview the full quiz exactly as a learner will see it before publishing.

subFeatures.name: Learner quiz experience
subFeatures.description: Quizzes run with timers, attempt limits, instant feedback, scorecards and a review mode inside the course player.

features.name: Assignments & Homework
features.tagline: Deadline-driven homework with submissions, marks and reports
features.description: Assignment slides give learners homework with a go-live date, deadline, total and passing marks, reference attachments and structured questions. Learners submit file uploads directly inside the course within precisely enforced submission windows, with configurable re-attempts. Staff review every submission per slide, spot late work, and export everything to CSV, while grades flow back to the learner's homework area.

subFeatures.name: Scheduling and live deadlines
subFeatures.description: Set a go-live date and due date per assignment; submission windows are enforced against server time with a live countdown, and late submissions are flagged.

subFeatures.name: Re-attempt policy
subFeatures.description: Allow a configurable number of resubmissions per learner; the app tracks attempts used and blocks further submissions once exhausted.

subFeatures.name: Marks and passing criteria
subFeatures.description: Define total and passing marks; learners see their graded marks and pass/fail outcome once evaluated.

subFeatures.name: Attachments and structured questions
subFeatures.description: Attach reference files and add structured questions with typed answers and options.

subFeatures.name: File-upload submissions
subFeatures.description: Learners upload one or more files as their submission, with allowed file types configurable by the teacher.

subFeatures.name: Submission review and CSV export
subFeatures.description: See every learner's submission in an activity sidebar and download all submissions as a CSV.

subFeatures.name: Homework list and reports
subFeatures.description: A dedicated homework area lists all pending and completed homework across courses, with report views summarizing submissions and outcomes over time.

features.name: Documents, PDFs & Presentation Decks
features.tagline: Rich docs, PDFs and animated slide decks learners love
features.description: Written content comes in every flavor: upload PDFs, convert Word documents into editable pages, author rich documents in a modern block editor, or let AI write the lesson for you. PowerPoint decks convert automatically — either into polished PDFs or into smooth animated web slideshows that replay build steps — so presentations look as good online as they did in class.

subFeatures.name: PDF slides
subFeatures.description: Upload PDFs with an in-app viewer; page counts are tracked so learner progress can be measured page by page.

subFeatures.name: Rich document editor
subFeatures.description: Create documents in a modern block-based editor with headings, lists, media and formatting; existing Word docs upload and convert to editable content.

subFeatures.name: AI-written documents
subFeatures.description: Ask AI to author or rewrite a document slide from a prompt — content arrives as a polished lesson you can keep refining with AI.

subFeatures.name: Animated PPT decks
subFeatures.description: Upload a .pptx and it becomes a web slideshow that preserves build-step animations as elegant cross-fades, played in an in-course deck player.

subFeatures.name: PowerPoint-to-PDF conversion
subFeatures.description: Classic .ppt and modern .pptx decks convert to PDFs through a professional-grade conversion engine with standard and high-quality modes; very large decks upload straight to cloud storage and convert by reference, sidestepping size limits.

subFeatures.name: Embedded URL slides
subFeatures.description: Embed any external web page or tool directly inside a chapter as its own slide.

features.name: Coding & STEM Slides
features.tagline: Runnable code, Jupyter notebooks and Scratch inside lessons
features.description: Teach programming inside the course itself. Code editor slides give learners an in-browser editor where they write and run code against the lesson's starter files; Jupyter notebook and Scratch project slides embed full interactive STEM environments. Every learner code submission is stored and reviewable by staff.

subFeatures.name: In-browser code editor slides
subFeatures.description: Author coding exercises with starter code; learners write and execute code in the browser with real language runtimes.

subFeatures.name: Jupyter notebook slides
subFeatures.description: Embed interactive Jupyter notebooks as lessons for data science and Python teaching.

subFeatures.name: Scratch project slides
subFeatures.description: Embed Scratch projects for block-based coding, ideal for younger learners.

subFeatures.name: Coding submission review
subFeatures.description: Every learner code submission is saved; staff can list submissions per slide and open any individual submission.

features.name: Quick Add & Bulk Content Upload
features.tagline: Publish a whole course of files in minutes
features.description: Two accelerators for content migration. Quick Add lets authors drop many files and links into a chapter at once — PDFs, docs, videos, audio, YouTube links, web links, code editors, notebooks and presentations — all published in one go. The Bulk Content Upload wizard ingests an entire zip or CSV manifest whose folder structure maps to Subjects, Modules and Chapters, previews the resulting course tree, then creates everything automatically — even across multiple courses in one upload.

subFeatures.name: Quick Add for chapters
subFeatures.description: Drag in multiple files and links; each becomes a correctly-typed, auto-titled, instantly published slide.

subFeatures.name: Zip-based course import
subFeatures.description: Upload a zip whose folders mirror your course depth (Subject/Module/Chapter); files inside become slides in the right place.

subFeatures.name: CSV manifest import
subFeatures.description: Alternatively drive the import from a CSV listing titles, types and URLs, with built-in validation results and help.

subFeatures.name: Multi-course upload
subFeatures.description: One zip can carry several courses at once, each in its own top-level folder.

subFeatures.name: Video-link list files
subFeatures.description: Plain text files of YouTube or video URLs expand into individual video slides with sensible titles.

subFeatures.name: Preview and issue checking
subFeatures.description: A preview tree shows exactly what will be created, with warnings for misplaced or skipped files before you commit.

subFeatures.name: Progress and results report
subFeatures.description: Watch the import run step by step and get a results summary of everything created.

subFeatures.name: Sample zip download
subFeatures.description: Download a sample zip that demonstrates the expected folder conventions.

features.name: Drip Content & Prerequisites
features.tagline: Unlock content by date, progress or prerequisites
features.description: Control exactly when learners can access content. Drip rules apply at course, chapter or individual-slide level and unlock content on a calendar date, after completion thresholds, after specific prerequisite chapters or slides, or strictly in sequence. Learners see locked items with clear unlock requirements, guiding them through a structured path that unlocks automatically as they progress.

subFeatures.name: Date-based unlock
subFeatures.description: Release a chapter or slide automatically on a chosen date and time.

subFeatures.name: Completion-based unlock
subFeatures.description: Unlock content when a learner reaches a completion threshold, including averages over the last N items.

subFeatures.name: Prerequisite rules
subFeatures.description: Require specific chapters or slides to be finished to a threshold before new content opens.

subFeatures.name: Sequential learning
subFeatures.description: Force learners to complete the previous item before moving on.

subFeatures.name: Three levels of control
subFeatures.description: Configure drip at whole-course, chapter or individual-slide level, each with its own dialog and enable toggle.

subFeatures.name: Locked badges and unlock requirements
subFeatures.description: Locked items show a lock indicator in the course tree, and learners see exactly what they must complete to unlock the next piece of content.

features.name: Certificates: Designer, Auto-Issue & Bulk Generation
features.tagline: Design once — certificates issue themselves
features.description: Design completion certificates visually with no designer needed: start from a template gallery, customize in a drag-and-drop editor or drop to HTML for full control, or upload your own PDF design and place data fields exactly where they belong. Certificates then issue automatically the moment a learner crosses the completion threshold — celebrated in-app with confetti, downloadable from the course page, and emailed with the PDF attached. Bulk generation personalizes hundreds of certificates at once from a batch or CSV.

subFeatures.name: Template gallery
subFeatures.description: Start from professionally designed built-in certificate templates.

subFeatures.name: Visual drag-and-drop editor
subFeatures.description: Design text, images and layout in a visual editor with a customization panel.

subFeatures.name: HTML mode
subFeatures.description: Drop to full HTML editing for pixel-perfect branded certificates.

subFeatures.name: Upload your own PDF design
subFeatures.description: Use an existing certificate design as the template by uploading it as a PDF.

subFeatures.name: Drag-and-drop field placement
subFeatures.description: Place name, email and any custom CSV column onto the exact spot on the certificate with a visual field palette.

subFeatures.name: Student data from batch or CSV
subFeatures.description: Pull learners from an enrolled batch or upload a CSV (with a downloadable template) and get validation results before generating.

subFeatures.name: Live per-student preview
subFeatures.description: Preview exactly how any individual learner's certificate will look before generation.

subFeatures.name: Bulk generation and download
subFeatures.description: Generate personalized certificates for a whole batch at once and download the PDFs plus a generation summary.

subFeatures.name: Auto-issue on course completion
subFeatures.description: Certificates generate automatically with the learner's name and course details once completion passes the institute-set threshold.

subFeatures.name: In-app celebration, view and download
subFeatures.description: A completion banner with confetti celebrates the milestone, and learners view and download their certificate from the course page.

subFeatures.name: Automatic certificate emails
subFeatures.description: Every issued certificate is recorded and delivered to the learner by branded email with the PDF attached.

features.name: Learner Home Dashboard
features.tagline: Everything a learner needs on one personalized screen
features.description: The learner's landing page brings together courses, live classes, tests, progress, attendance and announcements in one place. Institutes control exactly which widgets appear, so every brand shapes its own home experience — including a gamified 'Play' theme for younger audiences.

subFeatures.name: Continue Learning card
subFeatures.description: One-tap resume that takes the learner straight back to the exact lesson slide they last studied.

subFeatures.name: Learning stats cards
subFeatures.description: At-a-glance counters for enrolled courses, assigned tests and upcoming live sessions.

subFeatures.name: Upcoming live classes widget
subFeatures.description: Today's and upcoming live sessions with join buttons, all converted to the learner's own timezone.

subFeatures.name: Weekly attendance widget
subFeatures.description: The learner's live-class attendance for the week (present / absent / pending) right on the home screen.

subFeatures.name: Past learning insights
subFeatures.description: A 7-day activity view with charts showing how much the learner studied recently.

subFeatures.name: Pinned announcements panel
subFeatures.description: Institute announcements pinned by admins appear at the top of the dashboard.

subFeatures.name: Raise a query card
subFeatures.description: Learners can raise a support query to the institute directly from the dashboard.

subFeatures.name: Gamification panel
subFeatures.description: Streak counter, XP display, level progress ring and achievement badges appear when gamification is enabled.

subFeatures.name: Membership, books and orders widgets
subFeatures.description: Optional widgets showing the learner's active membership, purchased books and materials, and order history.

subFeatures.name: Institute-configurable widget layout
subFeatures.description: Admins choose per institute which dashboard widgets learners see, including alternative hero styles and a kid-friendly Play theme.

subFeatures.name: Terms acceptance prompt
subFeatures.description: A first-login modal captures the learner's acceptance of the institute's terms and conditions.

subFeatures.name: Notifications preview
subFeatures.description: Recent notifications listed on the dashboard with a link to the full notification center.

features.name: Course Approval Workflow
features.tagline: Teachers author, admins approve — quality stays controlled
features.description: Require editorial review before courses go live. Teachers author courses or create an editable copy of a live course, submit for review, and can withdraw a submission. Admins get a pending-review dashboard where they approve or reject with full course history and an approval summary — so published content is always vetted, and the live version stays untouched until approval.

subFeatures.name: Teacher-authored courses
subFeatures.description: Teachers create their own courses and track them in an authored-courses tab with detailed status.

subFeatures.name: Editable copies of live courses
subFeatures.description: Teachers edit a safe copy of a published course; the live version stays untouched until approval.

subFeatures.name: Submit and withdraw for review
subFeatures.description: Send a course for review or pull it back; an edit-permission check tells teachers when a course is locked.

subFeatures.name: Admin approval dashboard
subFeatures.description: Admins see all courses pending review, open full review details, and approve or reject with reasons.

subFeatures.name: Approval history and summary
subFeatures.description: Every course keeps an approval history, and a summary view shows the institute's review pipeline at a glance.

subFeatures.name: In-review protection
subFeatures.description: Slides under review are guarded against conflicting edits.

features.name: Content Copy & Reuse Across Batches
features.tagline: Build once, reuse everywhere — with lineage tracking
features.description: Copy whole course content between batches, sessions and courses, so new intakes start from proven material. The platform records copy lineage, showing where a batch's content originally came from, and copy or move is available at every level: course, session, subject, module, chapter and slide.

subFeatures.name: Copy whole course content
subFeatures.description: Duplicate a batch's full content tree into another batch or course in one operation.

subFeatures.name: Copy lineage badge
subFeatures.description: See the ancestry of copied content — which batch it was copied from — right on the course page.

subFeatures.name: Granular copy and move
subFeatures.description: Copy or move individual subjects, chapters and slides between any location in the library.

subFeatures.name: Copy content during course creation
subFeatures.description: Start a new course by copying content from an existing one via a guided dialog.

features.name: SCORM Course Import
features.tagline: Your existing SCORM e-learning works out of the box
features.description: Institutes with existing e-learning content can upload SCORM packages as slides. The platform hosts and plays each package and records learner progress and completion back into the course, so legacy content plugs straight into the modern LMS.

subFeatures.name: SCORM package upload
subFeatures.description: Upload a SCORM zip; it is unpacked, hosted and playable as a slide.

subFeatures.name: Progress tracking
subFeatures.description: Learner sessions record SCORM tracking data, so scores and completion flow into course progress.

subFeatures.name: SCORM preview
subFeatures.description: Authors preview the package inside the editor before publishing.

features.name: Audio Lessons
features.tagline: Podcast-style audio as first-class course content
features.description: Upload audio files as slides for language drills, lectures or podcast-style lessons. Audio plays in a dedicated in-course player with progress tracked like any other slide, and follows the same draft-and-publish lifecycle as all content.

subFeatures.name: Audio upload and player
subFeatures.description: Upload audio files which learners play in an in-course audio player with progress tracking.

subFeatures.name: Draft and publish for audio
subFeatures.description: Audio slides follow the same draft/publish lifecycle as other content.

features.name: Interactive Presentation & Whiteboard Slides
features.tagline: Draw, present and teach on an infinite canvas
features.description: Presentation slides open an infinite whiteboard canvas where teachers sketch diagrams, drawings and full slide decks. The same presentations can be used live through the presentation client, bridging course content and classroom teaching.

subFeatures.name: Whiteboard-style presentation editor
subFeatures.description: Create presentation slides on a freeform drawing canvas with shapes, text and sketches.

subFeatures.name: Create presentations from the library
subFeatures.description: Spin up a new interactive presentation directly from the study library for use in live teaching.

features.name: Study Material File Library
features.tagline: Organized folders of study docs with audience control
features.description: Beyond courses, staff maintain a folder-based library of standalone study documents. Upload files or create study docs in-app, organize them into folders, and control whether each document is visible to learners, staff only, or both.

subFeatures.name: Folders
subFeatures.description: Create, list and delete folders to organize study materials.

subFeatures.name: Upload or create study docs
subFeatures.description: Upload existing files or author new study documents directly in the app, singly or in bulk.

subFeatures.name: Audience-based access
subFeatures.description: Set each document's visibility to Learner, Admin or Both.

features.name: Course Settings & Authoring Policies
features.tagline: Institute-wide rules for how courses get built
features.description: Define how course creation behaves across the institute: which detail fields are required, the default and fixed course depth, whether sessions and levels are used, default view mode, and how finished courses reach the public catalogue. Per-course settings panels add workflow automation triggers, sub-organization sharing, offer pricing defaults and external LMS connections.

subFeatures.name: Required-field policies
subFeatures.description: Choose which course information fields are mandatory or enabled, from descriptions to banner images and promo media.

subFeatures.name: Structure defaults
subFeatures.description: Set the default course depth, lock it institute-wide, enable or disable sessions and levels, and set the default view mode and outline state.

subFeatures.name: Catalogue publishing mode
subFeatures.description: Decide whether finished courses go to the public catalogue automatically, manually, or with a prompt each time.

subFeatures.name: Learner creation permissions
subFeatures.description: Optionally allow learners to create courses, and control whether payment, discount and referral options can be changed per course.

subFeatures.name: Per-course settings editor
subFeatures.description: Override institute defaults for a single course through a dedicated settings panel.

subFeatures.name: Workflow triggers per course
subFeatures.description: Attach automation workflows to course events (e.g. on enrollment) and manage the triggers from the course settings panel.

subFeatures.name: Sub-organization sharing
subFeatures.description: Associate a course's batches with sub-organizations for franchise or multi-branch setups.

subFeatures.name: External LMS connections
subFeatures.description: Connect a course to an external LMS — LearnDash, Moodle or any custom LMS — with a saved connection library and one-click connection tests; Vacademy's built-in LMS needs no setup.

subFeatures.name: Offer pricing and rounding defaults
subFeatures.description: Configure offer pricing with automatic rounding rules for clean price points.

features.name: Content Protection
features.tagline: Keep your paid content from walking out the door
features.description: Per-role content protection controls make it harder to copy or leak your study material. Configure protections separately for Admins, Teachers and Learners, control who may download slides and documents role by role, and rely on in-viewer protection plus a mobile privacy screen against screenshots.

subFeatures.name: Per-role protection profiles
subFeatures.description: Enable protection rules separately for Admin, Teacher, Learner and other roles.

subFeatures.name: In-viewer copy protection
subFeatures.description: Protect slide content from copying inside the study viewer.

subFeatures.name: Slide download control
subFeatures.description: Decide role by role who may download slides and documents.

subFeatures.name: Mobile privacy screen
subFeatures.description: The mobile app adds a privacy screen that guards content against screenshots.

demo.intro: Follow a course from the admin's course builder to a learner finishing a lesson on their phone.

slide.title: In-video quiz checkpoint
slide.body: Question appears at 04:32 of the video — learners must answer before the lesson continues. Supports MCQ, numeric and one-word answers, with instant explanations.

steps.title: One library for every course
steps.text: Admins and teachers manage every offering here — courses, memberships, even one-off services. Live stats show enrollment, completion and ratings at a glance.

steps.title: Courses with a real lifecycle
steps.text: Each course moves through draft, editorial review and publish. Batches, levels and academic sessions hang off the same card — no duplicate content per batch.

steps.title: Create by hand or in bulk
steps.text: Start a course with the guided wizard, or bulk-upload existing folders of videos and PDFs — Vacademy maps them into chapters and slides automatically.

steps.title: Structure that matches how you teach
steps.text: Choose 2 to 5 levels of depth per course — from a flat chapter list up to Level → Session → Subject → Module → Chapter trees.

steps.title: A dozen slide types in one editor
steps.text: Mix video with in-video questions, PDFs, quizzes, code labs, whiteboards and homework in a single chapter. Slides keep full version history with restore.

steps.title: Publish with drip and controls
steps.text: Publish to specific batches, drip content on a schedule or after prerequisites, and control downloads and watermarking per course.

steps.title: The learner side, on any device
steps.text: Learners get the same course in a distraction-free player — web, Android app or desktop app — with resume, playback speed and offline-friendly viewing.

steps.title: Progress that drives itself
steps.text: True completion tracking (not just 'opened'), homework deadlines and newly unlocked tests surface right on the home screen.

steps.title: Momentum built in
steps.text: Streaks, XP and certificates keep learners coming back — and every bit of this activity feeds the reports admins see.

pillars.name: Assessments & Evaluation
pillars.tagline: Every exam format — online, offline, OMR — with manual and AI grading
pillars.description: A complete examination engine that takes institutes from question authoring to published results — online exams, mock tests, practice papers, homework, surveys and pen-and-paper tests all run on the same platform. Staff build papers from a rich question-type library or import them from Word, control exactly who takes each test, monitor exams live, and grade with manual tools or AI that evaluates handwritten answer sheets in minutes. Learners get a resilient exam experience on web, mobile and desktop, followed by detailed report cards with peer comparison and leaderboards.

features.name: Assessment Creation Wizard
features.tagline: Build any exam in four guided steps
features.description: A step-by-step wizard walks staff through creating an assessment: basic details, questions and sections, participants, and access control. It supports multiple assessment types — Exam, Mock, Practice, Survey and Manual-Upload exams where learners submit files instead of answering on screen — each with tailored options. Assessments stay in draft until published, so nothing goes live by accident.

subFeatures.name: Five assessment types
subFeatures.description: Choose between Exam, Mock test, Practice test, Survey, and Manual-Upload exam where learners submit PDFs, files or videos instead of answering on screen.

subFeatures.name: Basic details and instructions
subFeatures.description: Name the assessment, link it to a subject, and write rich-text instructions participants see before starting.

subFeatures.name: Live date window
subFeatures.description: Define the start and end date/time between which participants can begin and must finish.

subFeatures.name: Timed preview window
subFeatures.description: Optionally give participants a timed preview to read the paper before the clock starts.

subFeatures.name: Automatic or manual evaluation
subFeatures.description: Pick instant machine grading, or manual evaluation where teachers grade answer sheets.

subFeatures.name: Submission modes
subFeatures.description: Choose auto-submit when time expires or manual submit by the learner; manual-upload exams accept PDF, file, live-recorded video or uploaded video submissions.

subFeatures.name: Result release policy
subFeatures.description: Release results automatically after the exam ends, after each submission, or hold them for manual release.

subFeatures.name: Re-attempt settings
subFeatures.description: Set a default re-attempt count and optionally let learners raise re-attempt requests for admin approval.

subFeatures.name: Extra-time requests
subFeatures.description: Optionally let learners request more time during the exam, granted with admin consent.

subFeatures.name: Section switching control
subFeatures.description: Allow or block participants from moving back and forth between sections during the attempt.

subFeatures.name: OMR mode
subFeatures.description: Enable OMR-style answering for pen-and-paper tests whose responses are entered later.

subFeatures.name: Duration distribution
subFeatures.description: Apply the time limit at whole-assessment level, per section, or per question.

subFeatures.name: Draft / publish lifecycle
subFeatures.description: Assessments stay in draft while being built and publish only when ready; each wizard step tracks its own complete/incomplete status.

subFeatures.name: Public or private visibility
subFeatures.description: Mark an assessment public (open to anyone via link) or private (restricted to selected learners).

features.name: Rich Question Type Library
features.tagline: Nine question types, from MCQ to live coding
features.description: Author questions in a wide range of formats: single-correct MCQ, multiple-correct MCQ, true/false, one-word answer, long answer, fill-in-the-blank, match-the-following, numeric answer, and coding questions graded in the browser against test cases. Comprehension passages group related questions under one shared text, and every question supports formatted text, images and mathematical formulas.

subFeatures.name: MCQ — single correct
subFeatures.description: Classic multiple choice with one correct answer.

subFeatures.name: MCQ — multiple correct
subFeatures.description: Multiple-select questions with support for partial credit.

subFeatures.name: True / False
subFeatures.description: Quick binary questions.

subFeatures.name: One-word answer
subFeatures.description: Short free-text answers auto-checked against accepted words.

subFeatures.name: Long answer
subFeatures.description: Essay-style subjective questions with an answer key for evaluators, graded manually or by AI.

subFeatures.name: Numeric answer
subFeatures.description: Numeric questions with validation modes: single digit, any integer, positive integer, or decimal.

subFeatures.name: Fill in the blank
subFeatures.description: Sentence-completion questions with inline blanks.

subFeatures.name: Match the following
subFeatures.description: Pair items across two columns.

subFeatures.name: Coding questions
subFeatures.description: Learners write source code in an in-exam editor; submissions run against test cases and return a verdict with per-test-case results.

subFeatures.name: Comprehension groups
subFeatures.description: Attach a shared passage to sets of single-correct, multiple-correct or numeric questions.

subFeatures.name: Rich content everywhere
subFeatures.description: Question text, options and explanations support formatted text, images and mathematical formulas.

subFeatures.name: Per-question evaluation mode
subFeatures.description: Each question is marked for automatic or manual evaluation, so mixed papers grade the objective part instantly.

features.name: AI Answer-Sheet Evaluation
features.tagline: AI grades whole batches of answer sheets in minutes
features.description: Upload scanned answer sheets and let AI do the grading: the system reads each PDF — including handwriting and math — extracts every answer, applies your marking rubric, and awards question-wise marks with written feedback. Evaluate one learner or a whole classroom in a single run, watch progress live, and review or override any AI mark before results go out. Evaluation time drops from days to minutes while teachers keep the final say.

subFeatures.name: Rubric management
subFeatures.description: Create, edit and delete a marking rubric per assessment, down to individual questions, so the AI grades to your standard.

subFeatures.name: AI-drafted criteria and templates
subFeatures.description: Ask AI to draft evaluation criteria automatically, or manage a library of reusable criteria templates.

subFeatures.name: Batch evaluation
subFeatures.description: Select individual students or evaluate many at once, with live per-student status from pending through processing to completed.

subFeatures.name: Handwriting and math reading
subFeatures.description: The AI reads scanned PDFs including handwritten answers and mathematical notation, rendering math properly in the review view.

subFeatures.name: Live progress tracking
subFeatures.description: Watch each stage — PDF processing, answer extraction, criteria generation, grading, saving — with per-question statuses while evaluation runs.

subFeatures.name: Annotated answer sheets
subFeatures.description: The reviewed PDF shows AI annotations and margin notes anchored to the learner's actual writing.

subFeatures.name: Per-student summaries with remarks
subFeatures.description: Each student gets a consolidated score breakdown and written evaluation summary; evaluators can add their own remarks.

subFeatures.name: Human review and override
subFeatures.description: Teachers review each AI-graded question and adjust marks before release; a badge flags answers graded before a rubric change.

subFeatures.name: Stop, retry and run history
subFeatures.description: Stop a running evaluation, retry failures, and browse a history of every AI evaluation run per attempt with its status.

subFeatures.name: Evaluator AI quick portal
subFeatures.description: A focused portal for teachers who just want papers checked: a trimmed two-step builder for subjective papers, a student roster, batch evaluation and a guided first-time tour.

features.name: Manual Evaluation & Answer-Sheet Checking
features.tagline: Grade answer sheets on screen, attempt by attempt
features.description: A dedicated evaluation area lists every assessment awaiting grading and walks evaluators through attempts one learner at a time. Evaluators upload or view scanned answer sheets, annotate PDFs directly in the browser, award marks per question, and save partial work as drafts before final submission — with a built-in calculator and progress tracking along the way.

subFeatures.name: Evaluation queue
subFeatures.description: Live, upcoming and past tabs of assessments with a participant sidebar showing who still needs grading.

subFeatures.name: Answer sheet upload
subFeatures.description: Upload a learner's scanned answer sheet against their attempt for evaluation.

subFeatures.name: PDF annotation editor
subFeatures.description: Mark up scanned answer sheets directly in the browser, with the ability to reset annotations before confirming.

subFeatures.name: Per-question marks entry
subFeatures.description: Award marks question-by-question against the answer key and maximum marks, with totals computed automatically.

subFeatures.name: Built-in calculator
subFeatures.description: A calculator overlay for totalling marks without leaving the sheet.

subFeatures.name: Evaluation drafts
subFeatures.description: Save grading progress as a draft, resume later, or discard it.

subFeatures.name: Update marks and attempts
subFeatures.description: Correct previously submitted marks at question or attempt level.

subFeatures.name: All-attempts view
subFeatures.description: See every attempt of an assessment with its evaluation status in one list.

subFeatures.name: Evaluator progress tracking
subFeatures.description: See pages reviewed, questions not yet visited, total marks awarded and time spent on each evaluation.

subFeatures.name: Quick checking setup
subFeatures.description: Spin up a lightweight assessment with sections purely for answer-sheet checking, with AI-assisted publishing.

features.name: Learner Exam Experience
features.tagline: A serious exam hall on any device
features.description: Learners take scheduled and practice assessments in a purpose-built exam interface with sections, multi-level timers, a question palette and auto-submission. Answers stream continuously to the server, so crashes and flaky networks lose nothing, and interrupted attempts can be resumed. The same experience runs on web, mobile and desktop apps.

subFeatures.name: Exam catalog with tabs
subFeatures.description: Assessment lists organized into live, upcoming, past and attempted tabs, with search and pull-to-refresh.

subFeatures.name: Instructions and timed preview
subFeatures.description: Learners see exam details, duration, marking information and instructions before starting; where enabled, a timed preview lets them read the paper before the attempt clock starts.

subFeatures.name: Question navigator and list view
subFeatures.description: A palette showing answered, unanswered and marked questions with one-tap jumps, plus a scrollable full-paper list view.

subFeatures.name: Timers at every level
subFeatures.description: Assessment-level, section-level and per-question countdowns with automatic submission at expiry.

subFeatures.name: Controlled section switching
subFeatures.description: Multi-section papers where the institute decides whether learners may move between sections.

subFeatures.name: Autosave and network resilience
subFeatures.description: Answers and time state stream to the server throughout the attempt, with a live network status indicator protecting learners on unstable connections.

subFeatures.name: Resume interrupted attempts
subFeatures.description: If the app closes mid-exam, learners rejoin the running attempt and continue exactly where they were.

subFeatures.name: In-exam announcements
subFeatures.description: Admin broadcasts reach learners mid-exam without interrupting the attempt.

subFeatures.name: Homework and file submissions
subFeatures.description: Manual-upload assessments accept file, PDF or video submissions that learners mark complete.

subFeatures.name: Re-attempt and extra-time requests
subFeatures.description: Where allowed, learners request another attempt or more time and admins approve from the monitoring console.

subFeatures.name: Attention tracking
subFeatures.description: The exam screen records tab-switch and visibility events as part of the attempt data available to the institute.

features.name: Live Exam Monitoring & Submissions Console
features.tagline: Watch, extend, close and manage attempts in real time
features.description: While an exam runs, staff see participants grouped into Attempted, Ongoing and Pending tabs with live counters, search and filters. Individual and bulk actions cover the whole exam-day toolkit: extend time, force-close submissions, remove participants, send reminders, grant re-attempts and release results.

subFeatures.name: Attempt status tabs
subFeatures.description: Participants split into Attempted, Ongoing and Pending lists with a live summary strip and counters.

subFeatures.name: Increase time
subFeatures.description: Add extra minutes to ongoing attempts, individually or in bulk.

subFeatures.name: Close submissions
subFeatures.description: Force-submit ongoing attempts, individually or in bulk.

subFeatures.name: Grant re-attempts
subFeatures.description: Give selected learners another attempt after they have submitted.

subFeatures.name: Remove participants
subFeatures.description: Take pending participants off the exam roster in bulk.

subFeatures.name: Send reminders
subFeatures.description: Nudge pending participants who have not started yet.

subFeatures.name: Release results
subFeatures.description: Release results per learner, in bulk, or for the entire assessment when set to manual release.

subFeatures.name: Search and filters
subFeatures.description: Find participants by name and filter each tab before applying bulk actions.

features.name: Question Paper Bank
features.tagline: A searchable, taggable library of reusable papers
features.description: Every question paper lives in a central bank where staff can search, filter, favourite and reuse it across assessments. Papers are organised by level, subject and tags, and a public community bank lets institutes pull shared papers into their private collection with one click — protecting institute IP while making reuse effortless.

subFeatures.name: Create and edit papers
subFeatures.description: Author papers question-by-question in the editor, edit titles and metadata later, and preview the full paper at any time.

subFeatures.name: Filters and search
subFeatures.description: Filter the bank by level, subject and tags, search by name, and narrow by creation date range.

subFeatures.name: Favourites
subFeatures.description: Star frequently used papers into a separate Favourites view.

subFeatures.name: Question tags
subFeatures.description: Tag questions and papers for topic-wise organisation and retrieval.

subFeatures.name: Public question bank
subFeatures.description: Browse a public community bank of shared question papers with the same filters.

subFeatures.name: Copy public papers to private
subFeatures.description: One click imports a public paper into the institute's private bank for editing and use.

subFeatures.name: Archive papers
subFeatures.description: Remove outdated papers from the active bank without touching past assessments.

subFeatures.name: Question selector
subFeatures.description: Pick individual questions from existing papers when assembling a new assessment section.

features.name: Word / DOCX Question Import
features.tagline: Turn a Word file into a digital question paper
features.description: Upload a question paper written in Microsoft Word and the platform converts it into structured digital questions automatically — options, correct answers, explanations, tags, images and mathematical formulas included. Simple markers tell the importer where questions, options and answers begin, comprehension passages come across intact, and a diagnostics view flags anything that could not be parsed.

subFeatures.name: One-click DOCX conversion
subFeatures.description: Upload a .docx file and get back fully structured questions with options, answers and explanations, including multi-line question stems.

subFeatures.name: Configurable markers
subFeatures.description: Tell the importer which identifiers your paper uses for questions, options and answers so almost any format can be parsed.

subFeatures.name: Question type auto-detection
subFeatures.description: Based on the answer given, each question is classified automatically: single-choice MCQ, multiple-correct MCQ, numeric, one-word, or long-answer.

subFeatures.name: Comprehension passages
subFeatures.description: Passage-based sets with sub-questions are imported with the passage attached to every sub-question.

subFeatures.name: Auto-marking setup
subFeatures.description: Correct answers from the document become auto-evaluation rules, so imported papers are instantly auto-gradable.

subFeatures.name: Topic tags from the document
subFeatures.description: Tag lines around a question are captured as subject tags on that question, deduplicated automatically.

subFeatures.name: Images and formulas preserved
subFeatures.description: Embedded pictures, diagrams and equation images — including legacy Word formats — are converted to web-friendly formats and hosted so they render on any device.

subFeatures.name: Diagnostics and error reporting
subFeatures.description: Questions that break the expected format are flagged with a specific reason, so staff fix problems before saving instead of silently corrupting the paper.

subFeatures.name: Preview before save
subFeatures.description: Review and edit every imported question before the paper is added to the bank.

subFeatures.name: Downloadable format templates
subFeatures.description: A library of template files shows the exact document format to follow before uploading.

features.name: Sections & Marking Schemes
features.tagline: Flexible sections with fine-grained marking rules
features.description: Split any assessment into multiple sections, each with its own description, duration and marking rules. Marking is highly configurable — per-question marks, negative marking, partial credit, cutoff marks and question shuffling can all be set per section, with total marks calculated live as the paper is built.

subFeatures.name: Multiple sections
subFeatures.description: Add, reorder, describe and delete sections; attach a different question paper or question set to each.

subFeatures.name: Marks per question
subFeatures.description: Set default marks per question for a section, then fine-tune question-by-question with adaptive marking.

subFeatures.name: Negative marking
subFeatures.description: Switch on negative marking per section and set how many marks are deducted for wrong answers.

subFeatures.name: Partial marking
subFeatures.description: Award partial credit on multi-select questions where the learner gets some options right.

subFeatures.name: Cutoff marks
subFeatures.description: Define a per-section cutoff score for pass and qualification decisions.

subFeatures.name: Question randomization
subFeatures.description: Shuffle question order per participant to deter copying.

subFeatures.name: Section-wise duration
subFeatures.description: Give each section its own time budget when duration is distributed by section or question.

subFeatures.name: Live total-marks calculation
subFeatures.description: The builder totals marks across sections in real time so the paper always adds up.

features.name: Participant Management & Open Registration
features.tagline: Closed batch exams or open public tests
features.description: Decide exactly who can take each assessment. Closed tests are assigned to selected batches or individual learners; open tests get a shareable public link and QR code with a custom registration form, so anyone can sign up — ideal for scholarship tests and lead-generation exams. Registration windows, form fields and participant lists are all under staff control.

subFeatures.name: Closed tests by batch
subFeatures.description: Assign the assessment to one or more batches so every enrolled learner is pre-registered.

subFeatures.name: Closed tests by learner
subFeatures.description: Hand-pick individual learners from the institute roster.

subFeatures.name: Bulk batch registration
subFeatures.description: Register whole batches to an assessment in one operation.

subFeatures.name: Open public tests with link and QR
subFeatures.description: Generate a public join link and downloadable QR code so anyone can register and take the test.

subFeatures.name: Custom registration form
subFeatures.description: Build the open-registration form with text and dropdown fields, mark fields required, and drag to reorder them.

subFeatures.name: Registration window
subFeatures.description: Set registration open and close dates independently of the exam window, plus registration instructions.

subFeatures.name: Public assessment page
subFeatures.description: Every open assessment gets a shareable page, addressed by a short code, showing the exam name, schedule and instructions where prospects self-register instantly.

subFeatures.name: Participant status check
subFeatures.description: Registrants can look up whether their registration and attempt are confirmed.

subFeatures.name: Registered participants list
subFeatures.description: View, filter and export everyone registered, including their registration source: admin pre-registration, batch, or open link.

subFeatures.name: Expected participants
subFeatures.description: Record expected participant counts for planning open tests.

features.name: Learner Results & Report Cards
features.tagline: Rich post-exam reports learners can download
features.description: After results release, learners get a detailed report: score breakdown, peer comparison, leaderboard position, and how the rest of the cohort answered each question. Reports download as formatted PDFs, or as AI-narrated report cards with written feedback on the attempt.

subFeatures.name: Report list and detail
subFeatures.description: Learners see all their assessment reports and open question-wise detail for any attempt.

subFeatures.name: Peer comparison
subFeatures.description: Compare personal performance against batch averages and toppers.

subFeatures.name: Leaderboard
subFeatures.description: Where enabled by the institute, learners view the assessment leaderboard.

subFeatures.name: Option distribution
subFeatures.description: See what percentage of peers picked each option on every question.

subFeatures.name: PDF report download
subFeatures.description: Download the report as a formatted PDF.

subFeatures.name: AI report card
subFeatures.description: Generate an AI-written PDF report with narrative feedback on the attempt.

features.name: Re-evaluation Engine
features.tagline: Regrade anything after marking-scheme changes
features.description: If an answer key or marking rule changes after the exam, staff trigger re-evaluation at any granularity: the entire assessment, selected participants, or specific participants on specific questions. Every evaluation change is recorded in an audit log for accountability.

subFeatures.name: Full assessment re-evaluation
subFeatures.description: Regrade every attempt of the assessment in one action.

subFeatures.name: Participant-level re-evaluation
subFeatures.description: Regrade only the selected learners' attempts.

subFeatures.name: Question-wise re-evaluation
subFeatures.description: Regrade chosen questions for chosen participants — ideal after an answer-key correction.

subFeatures.name: Evaluation audit log
subFeatures.description: A per-assessment log records manual evaluation and regrade events for accountability.

features.name: Offline / OMR Response Entry
features.tagline: Digitise pen-and-paper exam responses fast
features.description: For exams conducted on paper, staff enter learner responses into the platform afterwards and get the full digital stack — grading, leaderboards and reports — as if the exam were online. Entry works question-by-question or in a fast spreadsheet-style table view.

subFeatures.name: Create offline attempts
subFeatures.description: Pick a learner and create an attempt on their behalf for the offline exam.

subFeatures.name: Question-by-question entry
subFeatures.description: A question navigator with a response form for careful data entry, rendering the original rich question content.

subFeatures.name: Table entry mode
subFeatures.description: A spreadsheet-like grid to enter many responses quickly.

subFeatures.name: One-shot create-and-submit
subFeatures.description: Create the attempt and submit all responses in a single step.

subFeatures.name: Automatic grading of entered data
subFeatures.description: Entered responses are graded by the same engine, feeding results, ranks and analytics.

subFeatures.name: Institute-level offline entry setting
subFeatures.description: Turn manual marks entry for offline and paper assessments on or off across the institute.

features.name: Surveys & Feedback Forms
features.tagline: Collect and analyse responses like a survey pro
features.description: The same builder powers unscored surveys: send questionnaires to batches or the public and analyse results in a dedicated dashboard. Staff view aggregate distributions per question, drill into individual respondents, and export the respondent list.

subFeatures.name: Survey builder
subFeatures.description: Create surveys with the standard question types, sections, open or closed distribution and custom registration forms — with marking hidden since surveys are unscored.

subFeatures.name: Survey overview analytics
subFeatures.description: Per-question response distributions with charts and completion metrics.

subFeatures.name: Question-wise responses
subFeatures.description: Inspect all answers to a single question across respondents.

subFeatures.name: Respondent-wise view
subFeatures.description: Open one respondent and read their entire submission.

subFeatures.name: Respondent exports
subFeatures.description: Export the respondent list as CSV or PDF.

subFeatures.name: Survey access control
subFeatures.description: Separate staff access lists for survey creation, live notifications, submissions and reports.

features.name: Question Paper Export & Printing
features.tagline: Print-ready papers with your formatting rules
features.description: Export any question paper or assessment as a print-ready PDF for offline exams. Layout, typography and answer-space options are configurable, and multiple paper sets can be produced for anti-cheating distribution in exam halls.

subFeatures.name: Print settings
subFeatures.description: Control layout, font size and whether images keep their aspect ratio.

subFeatures.name: Custom header fields
subFeatures.description: Add custom input fields such as name and roll number, plus an optional question-set code, to the printed paper.

subFeatures.name: Answer spacing
subFeatures.description: Configure blank answer space under each question for written exams.

subFeatures.name: Multiple paper sets
subFeatures.description: Generate several sets of the same paper for anti-cheating distribution in exam halls.

subFeatures.name: Preview and export
subFeatures.description: Preview the final formatted paper on screen before downloading the PDF.

features.name: Exam Notifications & Reminders
features.tagline: Automatic alerts to learners and parents
features.description: The platform notifies participants — and optionally their parents — at every stage of an assessment: when it is created, shortly before it goes live, the moment it starts, and when reports are ready. Scheduled jobs send these automatically across email, WhatsApp and push, and staff can fire manual reminders to learners who have not attempted yet.

subFeatures.name: Learner notifications
subFeatures.description: Opt-in notifications when the assessment is created, before it goes live with a configurable lead time, when it goes live, and when the report is generated.

subFeatures.name: Parent notifications
subFeatures.description: The same notification set can be sent to parents independently of learners.

subFeatures.name: Automated scheduling
subFeatures.description: An hourly scheduler sends upcoming and started notifications without any manual work.

subFeatures.name: Manual reminders
subFeatures.description: Select pending participants who have not attempted and send them a reminder in bulk.

subFeatures.name: Multi-channel delivery
subFeatures.description: Notifications go out over email, WhatsApp and push notifications.

subFeatures.name: Result notification recipients
subFeatures.description: Configure which recipients — learner, parents, staff — are notified when results release.

features.name: Assessment Access Control & Collaboration
features.tagline: Decide which staff can create, watch and grade
features.description: Per-assessment permissions let institutes split duties across the team. Separate access lists control who can edit the assessment, who gets live-exam alerts, who can see submissions and reports, and who participates in evaluation — assigned by role or by inviting individual staff.

subFeatures.name: Creation access
subFeatures.description: Grant specific roles or users the right to edit the assessment setup.

subFeatures.name: Live notification access
subFeatures.description: Choose who is alerted while the exam is running.

subFeatures.name: Submission and report access
subFeatures.description: Control who can open submissions and analytics for each assessment.

subFeatures.name: Evaluation access
subFeatures.description: Assign the evaluators allowed to grade answer sheets.

subFeatures.name: Invite institute users
subFeatures.description: Search existing staff by role and add them to any access list; filter and revoke invitations.

features.name: Assessments & Quizzes Inside Courses
features.tagline: Full tests and instant quizzes in the course flow
features.description: Embed a complete test from the assessment engine into any course chapter — link an existing assessment or create a new one without leaving the course editor — and review learner submissions right beside the content. Chapter quizzes give learners timers, attempt limits, instant scorecards and a full answer-review mode.

subFeatures.name: Link existing assessment
subFeatures.description: Place any assessment already built in the institute as a slide in the course.

subFeatures.name: Create assessment in place
subFeatures.description: Open a create-assessment form from within the course editor and attach the new test immediately.

subFeatures.name: Submissions panel
subFeatures.description: Review learner attempts and results for the embedded assessment directly from the slide view.

subFeatures.name: Quiz timer with warnings
subFeatures.description: Countdown timer with an on-screen warning; the quiz auto-submits when time expires.

subFeatures.name: Attempt limits
subFeatures.description: Cap re-attempts per quiz; once attempts are exhausted the learner moves to review mode.

subFeatures.name: Instant scorecard
subFeatures.description: A score summary appears immediately after submission.

subFeatures.name: Answer review mode
subFeatures.description: Learners revisit each question to see their answer, the correct answer and explanations.

subFeatures.name: Attempt history
subFeatures.description: Past attempts are stored and viewable, so learners can track improvement.

features.name: Assessment & Homework Organization
features.tagline: Every test and homework organised by status at a glance
features.description: The assessments home organises everything into Live, Upcoming, Previous and Draft tabs with search, filters and per-row quick actions. A dedicated homework area gives the same status-based view for homework tests, with creation running through the standard wizard pre-set to homework mode.

subFeatures.name: Status tabs
subFeatures.description: Live, Upcoming, Previous and Draft tabs keep the assessment list manageable.

subFeatures.name: Filters and search
subFeatures.description: Filter by batch, subject and other attributes; search assessments by name.

subFeatures.name: Quick actions menu
subFeatures.description: Per-assessment menu for editing, viewing details and submissions, exporting the paper, and deleting.

subFeatures.name: Detail tabs
subFeatures.description: Each assessment opens into Basic Info, Questions, Participants, Access Control and Submissions tabs, including a learner-view preview of the paper.

subFeatures.name: Homework list with status tabs
subFeatures.description: Browse homework tests split into upcoming, live and past states with per-tab counts, search and filter chips.

subFeatures.name: Create homework via wizard
subFeatures.description: Launch the multi-step assessment creation flow pre-set to homework mode, with schedule details and quick actions on every item.

demo.intro: Build a NEET mock test in the wizard, watch the exam hall live, sit the paper as a learner, and let AI grade the answer sheets.

fields.label: Evaluation
fields.value: Automatic · release results after exam ends

Match each physical quantity with its SI unit
A projectile fired at 45° covers what range (in m)?
Explain Kirchhoff's junction rule with one example

items.title: Q34 · Physics
items.sub: A 2 kg block slides down a 30° frictionless incline. Find its acceleration in m/s².

items.title: Priya Sharma
items.sub: Long answers graded · Q4 flagged for review

steps.title: Five exam types, four guided steps
steps.text: One wizard builds Exams, Mock tests, Practice tests, Surveys and Manual-Upload exams where learners submit PDFs or videos. Everything stays in draft until you publish, so nothing goes live by accident.

steps.title: Exam-day rules, decided upfront
steps.text: Set the live window, timed preview, auto- or manual submission, and whether results release automatically or on your say-so. Re-attempt and extra-time requests can be switched on for admin approval.

steps.title: Nine question types, or import Word
steps.text: Mix MCQs, numeric, match-the-following, comprehension passages, even coding questions run against test cases. Or upload a .docx paper — options, answers, images and math formulas convert automatically.

steps.title: See the whole exam hall live
steps.text: While the exam runs, participants split into Attempted, Ongoing and Pending tabs with live counters, search and filters — 186 learners, one glance.

steps.title: Extend, close, remind — in bulk
steps.text: Approve Priya's extra-time request, force-close stragglers, remove no-shows, grant re-attempts and nudge pending learners with reminders — per learner or in bulk, without leaving this console.

steps.title: Timers that never lose an answer
steps.text: Countdowns run at exam, section and question level with auto-submit at expiry. Answers stream to the server continuously — a crash or dropped network loses nothing, and learners resume exactly where they were.

steps.title: A palette built for exam strategy
steps.text: Answered, marked and skipped questions are one tap away, and the institute controls whether learners can move between sections. Tab-switch events are recorded with the attempt for the institute to review.

steps.title: AI grades a classroom in minutes
steps.text: Upload scanned answer sheets and AI reads them — handwriting and math included — applies your rubric, and awards question-wise marks with written feedback. Watch per-student status live, from processing to completed.

steps.title: Teachers keep the final say
steps.text: Every AI mark can be reviewed and overridden, with annotated PDFs anchored to the learner's actual writing. Changed the answer key? Re-evaluate the whole exam or single questions, with an audit log of every regrade.

steps.title: Results learners actually read
steps.text: Release results in bulk or per learner. Each learner gets peer comparison, option-wise distributions, a leaderboard rank and a downloadable AI-narrated report card — with alerts to parents over email, WhatsApp and push.

pillars.name: Live Classroom
pillars.tagline: Schedule, teach and engage live — built-in classroom or Zoom/Meet
pillars.description: Everything an institute needs to run live teaching at scale: schedule one-time or recurring classes in minutes, host them on the built-in classroom or the platforms you already use (Zoom, Google Meet, Zoho, YouTube, custom links), and keep learners inside your branded app from waiting room to replay. Volt, the built-in live presentation studio, turns every lecture into an interactive show with polls, leaderboards, whiteboards and an AI co-pilot. Attendance, recordings and post-class feedback are captured automatically, so nothing about a class needs manual follow-up.

features.name: Live Class Scheduling
features.tagline: One-time or recurring live classes, scheduled in minutes
features.description: A guided two-step wizard schedules live classes across batches — one-time sessions or weekly recurring series with per-day timings, subjects, rich descriptions and cover images. Classes can be bulk-imported, saved as drafts, previewed exactly as learners will see them, and rescheduled or cancelled with automatic learner notifications.

subFeatures.name: Two-step scheduling wizard
subFeatures.description: Step one sets class basics — title, subject, description, platform and timing; step two adds participants, registration options and notifications before publishing.

subFeatures.name: One-time and weekly recurring classes
subFeatures.description: Schedule a single class or a weekly repeating series with an end date; each weekday can carry multiple sessions with its own start time, duration and link.

subFeatures.name: Per-day class configuration
subFeatures.description: In recurring schedules, each day of the week can have its own class link, name, thumbnail and custom learner action button.

subFeatures.name: Timezone-aware scheduling
subFeatures.description: Every class is scheduled in an explicit timezone, so institutes with learners in multiple regions always see correct local times.

subFeatures.name: Bulk scheduling via CSV import
subFeatures.description: Upload a spreadsheet to create many classes at once, with a validation grid to review and fix rows before submitting.

subFeatures.name: Draft classes
subFeatures.description: Save partially configured classes as drafts and finish them later from a dedicated Drafts tab.

subFeatures.name: Live / Upcoming / Past / Drafts session list
subFeatures.description: One list of all classes filtered by state, with search and pagination.

subFeatures.name: Calendar view
subFeatures.description: See every scheduled class on a calendar for quick visual planning.

subFeatures.name: Class preview
subFeatures.description: Preview exactly what learners will see for a scheduled class before it goes live.

subFeatures.name: Custom learner action button
subFeatures.description: Add a fully branded button — custom text, URL and colors — to the learner's class card, such as 'Download worksheet' or 'Join WhatsApp group'.

subFeatures.name: Cancel with learner notification
subFeatures.description: Delete single occurrences or whole series and optionally notify enrolled learners automatically.

subFeatures.name: Class cover, thumbnail and waiting-room music
subFeatures.description: Attach a cover image, thumbnail and background audio track that plays in the waiting room for a polished pre-class experience.

subFeatures.name: Search and audit trail
subFeatures.description: Search across all sessions, and review each class's log of actions — created, starting, started, rescheduled — for accountability.

features.name: Meeting Platform Integrations
features.tagline: Zoom, Google Meet, Zoho, YouTube or any link — your choice
features.description: Connect the video platforms your institute already uses and let Vacademy create meetings automatically. Zoom (with multiple accounts), Google Meet, Zoho Meeting, YouTube live streams and any custom meeting link are all supported, and entire recurring series are provisioned in the background with retry protection. Recordings and attendance flow back automatically via provider webhooks.

subFeatures.name: Multi-account Zoom
subFeatures.description: Connect several Zoom accounts, set a default, test each connection, and pick which account hosts each class.

subFeatures.name: Full Zoom meeting controls from inside Vacademy
subFeatures.description: Configure waiting room, join-before-host, required Zoom login, registration approval, alternative hosts, mute on entry, host and participant video, audio mode (computer, telephony or both), automatic cloud or local recording, breakout rooms, focus mode, multi-device join and identity watermark — all while scheduling.

subFeatures.name: Google Meet integration
subFeatures.description: Connect one or more Google Workspace accounts via secure Google sign-in, set a default, test the connection and auto-create Meet links for classes; disconnect access at any time.

subFeatures.name: Zoho Meeting integration
subFeatures.description: Connect Zoho via secure sign-in; meetings, attendance and recordings sync automatically on a schedule.

subFeatures.name: YouTube live and custom links
subFeatures.description: Paste a YouTube live URL or any meeting link — Teams, Webex and more — when you prefer to manage meetings yourself.

subFeatures.name: Automatic meeting provisioning for series
subFeatures.description: When a recurring class is created, meetings for every occurrence are created in the background, with a provisioning status view, a manual 'provision now' action and automatic retry.

subFeatures.name: Host and participant links kept separate
